covered_report_window: Weekend actionable window: companies reporting after the U.S. market close on Friday, July 17, 2026, plus companies reporting before the U.S. market open on Monday, July 20, 2026.
market_context_summary: Friday's U.S. tape was risk-off: the Nasdaq fell about 1.4%, the S&P 500 about 1.0%, chips/AI names remained under pressure, Netflix weakness weighed on growth sentiment, and oil stayed elevated on Middle East risk. That makes forward guidance, consumer demand, fuel sensitivity, and balance-sheet quality especially important for the Monday pre-open earnings window.

top_setups.screening_note: The ranked universe was limited to DPZ, AMC, RYAAY, and DX after strict timing review. Monday after-close names such as STLD, WRB, CCK, ZION, AGNC, BOKF, CALX, WTFC, MCRI, and SFBS were outside this weekend window. SMBK was excluded because current-cycle timing was unclear or conflicting across sources.

ranked_companies.timing: Before market open, Monday, July 20, 2026; results expected around 6:05 a.m. ET and webcast at 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: U.S. same-store sales and 2026 comp tone
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: Price-war pressure or no forward-demand lift
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: DPZ has the cleanest qualified setup because expectations were reset by the Q1 miss and pizza price-war narrative, while order-count, share, margin, and buyback support leave room for relief if Q2 comps hold up.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Earnings Whispers listed DPZ at 6:05 a.m. ET with EPS around $4.09 and revenue around $1.18B; Investing.com showed a similar $4.16 EPS and $1.18B revenue forecast. The market is likely focused more on U.S. comps, order count, international comps, and forward comp commentary than headline EPS alone.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Positive U.S. order-count/share commentary, stronger menu/value traction, supply-chain margin resilience, and continued buybacks could drive a relief reaction.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: A merely in-line quarter with low-single-digit comps, international softness, or unchanged/soft forward demand could disappoint after any pre-event optimism.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Public pre-event sources showed meaningful event attention; social read was mixed but not extreme. The setup looks less crowded than a high-multiple growth name, but the implied move still requires a clean comp/tone read.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 missed published EPS expectations and triggered a price-war/consumer caution narrative; Q2 consensus clustered near $4.09-$4.16 EPS and $1.18B revenue.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes leaned up, with one more cautious due to forward-guide risk.
ranked_companies.method_update_note: Existing forward-demand allocation guardrail applied: DPZ qualified only partially because the bullish case still needs comp and demand confirmation.

ranked_companies.timing: Before market open, Monday, July 20, 2026; webcast 7:30 a.m. CDT / 8:30 a.m. EDT
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Q2 free cash flow and liquidity/debt update
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: AMC leans up because Q2 box-office strength and recent balance-sheet actions create a low-bar relief setup, but reversal risk is high because debt, dilution, and retail-positioning dynamics can overpower headline improvement.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company timing is confirmed for July 20 with an 8:30 a.m. EDT webcast; calendar estimates clustered around breakeven EPS and roughly $1.45B-$1.49B revenue. The market is likely to focus on cash generation, debt runway, attendance, per-patron spend, and liquidity after recent equity raises.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Strong Q2 attendance, positive free cash flow, higher cash, no near-term maturities, and upbeat film-slate commentary could trigger relief buying.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: New dilution, ongoing leverage, weak per-patron spend, or guidance that implies Q2 is a temporary box-office peak could sink the stock despite a better quarter.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: AMC remains a high-volume retail and meme-sensitive name; Friday volume was about 41.2M shares and the stock fell 6.28%, which increases both relief and reversal risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Consensus-style expectations were low, but the quality of earnings and cash flow matters more than the EPS line because the balance sheet remains central.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes leaned up, but all flagged high opposite-direction risk.
ranked_companies.method_update_note: Existing reversal-risk guardrail applied: strong box-office thesis was not allowed into allocation because dilution and leverage risk stayed high.

ranked_companies.timing: Expected before U.S. market open, Monday, July 20, 2026; company IR confirms July 20 Q1 FY27 event and high-quality calendars list before-open timing
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Q1 fare/yield and FY27 traffic tone
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: Less-bad fare guide or booking relief
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: RYAAY leans down because the market is likely to test Q1 fare pressure, Boeing delivery constraints, and fuel/geopolitical risk after a strong FY26 baseline, though a low-cost relief beat could reverse the move.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Investing.com listed July 20 estimates near $1.40 EPS and $5.11B revenue; MarketBeat showed before-open timing and about $1.37 EPS / $5.06B revenue. The key metric is fare/yield and forward traffic/capacity guidance, not EPS alone.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Close-in bookings, fare commentary, fuel hedging, or cost advantage could make the expected Q1 fare weakness look already priced in.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Mid-digit fare declines, Boeing delivery delays, capacity limits, or higher fuel/oil risk could pressure the stock even if EPS clears consensus.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Friday's ADR decline of about 5.7% de-risked some downside but also reflected investor concern around travel/fuel and forward fare risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: FY26 results were strong, but FY27 setup includes slower traffic growth and expected Q1 fare pressure, creating a tougher expectation mix.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes were neutral-to-down; one pass stayed closer to neutral due to relief potential after the Friday selloff.
ranked_companies.method_update_note: Existing forward-demand guardrail applied: fare/yield confirmation was weighted above headline EPS.

ranked_companies.timing: Before market open, Monday, July 20, 2026; call 10:00 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.evidence_quality: High for timing; Medium for direction
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: DX lacks a directional edge because dividend and net-interest support are balanced by book-value volatility, ATM dilution, leverage, and sensitivity to mortgage spreads.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Dynex officially scheduled Q2 results before the July 20 open; MarketBeat listed consensus around $0.36 EPS and $103.62M revenue. Investors likely care most about book value, economic return, leverage, liquidity, and dividend coverage.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Book value recovered after Q1, net interest income covers the dividend, and management frames MBS spreads as attractive.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Another book-value decline, higher leverage, or rate/spread volatility would undercut dividend support.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Event reaction is likely to track book-value/rate signals more than social sentiment; public social attention was limited.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 showed a negative 2.5% economic return and $12.60 book value, but some post-quarter commentary pointed to book-value improvement and rising net interest income.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes clustered near neutral, with one mild-down pass and two mild-up passes.

qualified_names.why: Only qualified name with absolute score above 30, reversal risk at or below 40, medium confidence, high-quality timing/evidence, and low pass disparity. Allocation remains partial because the bullish case still needs Q2 comp and forward-demand confirmation.

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Score was strong enough, but reversal risk was high due to dilution, leverage, meme positioning, and balance-sheet sensitivity.
Lean Down score cleared 30 in absolute terms, but reversal risk was moderate-high because Friday's selloff and low-cost cost advantage create relief potential.
Consensus score was near neutral and direction depends heavily on rate and MBS-spread marks.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Pass A/B/C averaged to +31 direction score, 39 reversal risk, and 12 disparity.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: All passes leaned up; one was more cautious because a clean quarter without better comp tone may not be enough.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Up; highest research-priority name in the qualified window, but partial allocation only.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: Reset expectations plus market-share/order-count resilience create relief potential if Q2 comps and tone are better than the price-war narrative implies.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: About $4.09-$4.16 EPS and $1.18B revenue, with focus on U.S. comps, international comps, supply-chain margin, and 2026 demand tone.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Positive U.S. order count, stronger value/menu traction, stable international comps, better supply-chain margins, and buyback support.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Low-single-digit comps with no forward-demand lift, ongoing pizza discount pressure, weak international comps, or a guide that implies slowing traffic.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Moderate-low but not trivial; the June 3 lesson raised caution because bullish names that need a forward-demand raise can reverse even on a headline beat.
detailed_company_sections.social_consumer_sentiment_read: Consumer narrative is mixed: value and digital strengths remain, but public reporting emphasizes a pizza price war and cautious customers.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Event attention is meaningful but not obviously euphoric; the implied-move hurdle still requires a comp/tone surprise rather than EPS alone.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: The Q1 miss and price-war coverage lowered the bar; Q2 estimates are clustered and leave room for relief if operational KPIs improve.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Lean-up reaction on stable U.S. comps and constructive demand commentary.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Clear share gains plus stronger forward comp language trigger a bigger relief move.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: The market treats Q2 as in-line and sells any sign that demand remains promotion-dependent.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Qualified for 65% of the notional research basket; low disparity and sufficient evidence helped, but reversal risk kept 35% unallocated.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Pass A/B/C averaged to +37 direction score, 62 reversal risk, and 19 disparity.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: All passes leaned up, but all treated the opposite-direction move as a real risk.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Up for direction; exclude from allocation because reversal risk is high.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: The box-office and cash-flow setup is better than usual for AMC, but the balance sheet and dilution overhang make the reaction unusually unstable.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: Roughly breakeven EPS and $1.45B-$1.49B revenue; market focus should be free cash flow, liquidity, maturities, attendance, per-patron spend, and debt actions.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Positive FCF, higher cash, strong Q2 attendance, debt maturity relief, and upbeat summer film-slate commentary.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Dilution, leverage reminders, weak profitability quality, or management commentary implying Q2 strength is temporary.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: High; a good quarter can still fall if shareholders focus on dilution/debt or if retail enthusiasm fades after the event.
detailed_company_sections.social_consumer_sentiment_read: Retail attention is still high and the 2026 box-office narrative is constructive; meme dynamics make sentiment supportive but noisy and manipulable.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Very active trading and meme sensitivity can amplify both directions; Friday's 6.28% decline increases relief potential but also signals fragility.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: The earnings bar is low, but sell-side style estimates are less important than free cash flow and balance-sheet credibility.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Lean-up relief on Q2 cash generation and liquidity improvement.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Clear positive free cash flow plus no-near-term-maturity messaging drives a squeeze.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Dilution/debt concerns dominate and the stock sells despite better revenue.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded from allocation because reversal risk was 62, above the 40 cutoff, despite a positive direction score.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Pass A/B/C averaged to -30 direction score, 49 reversal risk, and 15 disparity.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: All passes were neutral-to-down; one was milder because Friday's ADR selloff may have lowered the bar.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: The stock faces a fare/yield and fuel-risk test after a strong FY26, and the market may punish any evidence that Q1 pricing or Boeing-constrained capacity is softer than expected.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: About $1.37-$1.40 EPS and $5.06B-$5.11B revenue; key driver is Q1 fare/yield and FY27 traffic/capacity guidance.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Less-bad fares, strong close-in bookings, favorable fuel hedge commentary, or reassurance that cost advantage offsets capacity constraints.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Mid-digit fare declines, Boeing delivery constraints, weaker traffic guide, or higher fuel/geopolitical cost pressure.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Moderate-high; the ADR fell sharply Friday and Ryanair's cost advantage can create fast relief if management sounds confident.
detailed_company_sections.social_consumer_sentiment_read: Consumer demand backdrop looks resilient but fare-sensitive; social evidence was not a primary driver.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: The Friday selloff suggests some de-risking; reversal risk remains because consensus is already looking for fare pressure.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: FY26 results were strong, but Q1 FY27 is measured against a tough fare recovery comparison and slower traffic-growth expectations.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Lean-down reaction if fare commentary confirms pressure.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Bookings and cost control make the fare decline look priced in.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Guidance cuts traffic/yield confidence and oil risk compounds the move.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because reversal risk was 49 and timing/evidence quality was less clean than DPZ/AMC/DX.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Pass A/B/C averaged to +5 direction score, 39 reversal risk, and 17 disparity.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: All passes stayed close to neutral; the mild direction varied with book-value assumptions.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: Dividend support and possible post-Q1 book-value improvement are offset by mortgage-spread volatility, leverage, and ATM dilution risk.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: About $0.36 EPS and $103.62M revenue, but investors likely care most about book value, economic return, leverage, liquidity, and dividend coverage.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Book value recovery, net interest income covering the dividend, and constructive MBS-spread deployment commentary.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Another book-value decline, leverage concern, ATM issuance drag, or rate/spread volatility.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-to-moderate; the base call is neutral, so the key risk is a non-earnings rate/spread move creating a larger reaction than fundamentals imply.
detailed_company_sections.social_consumer_sentiment_read: Limited social signal; not relevant enough to move the score.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Event likely follows book-value and rate marks more than options or retail sentiment.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Q1 had negative economic return and book-value pressure; later commentary suggested possible book-value improvement and rising net interest income.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Muted reaction if book value and dividend coverage are broadly in line.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Book value recovery and dividend coverage trigger yield-buyer support.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Book-value loss or spread volatility overwhelms the yield story.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Neutral / No Edge, score +5, confidence Medium-Low.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because the direction score was far below 30.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
